reason: Web sources directly corroborate the scorer's core claims. Screen Rant explicitly notes that 'Frank puts up a fight in the book, coming across as courageous rather than merely looking frail and lost as he does on the big screen.' The HP fandom wiki confirms his verbal confrontation with Voldemort exists in the book ('Turn round and face me like a man, why don't you?') but the film strips this out. The structural facts are all accurate: he investigates a light, he is killed by Voldemort, and the film removes his defiant confrontation. The score of 57/100 is reasonable for a character whose structural role is preserved but whose defining personality moment is cut. Minor uncertainty: the film does give him slightly more than just 'bloody kids' - TV Tropes notes he delivers a sarcastic line to Voldemort in the film - so the claim that his verbal confrontation is entirely absent may be slightly overstated, which is why this isn't a 10.
